# Extend McpServerToolLimits test to all MCP servers

### Context
Currently, the `McpServerToolLimits.spec.mjs` test only applies to the `memory-core` MCP server. We need to enforce the 1024-character description limit across all MCP servers to catch bloated tool descriptions before they reach production. 

### The Problem
During PR #10832 review, it was discovered that three `gh-workflow` tools are currently over the 1024-character limit (`sync_all` 1627, `update_issue_relationship` 1430, `list_pull_requests` 1025). The lack of cross-server testing allows this technical debt to accumulate.

### The Architectural Reality
The test logic exists in `ai/test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/McpServerToolLimits.spec.mjs`. It needs to be refactored or extended to iterate over all five MCP servers in the repository (or whichever servers define tools).

### The Fix
1. Move or refactor the `McpServerToolLimits.spec.mjs` test to iterate over the `openapi.yaml` (or equivalent definition surfaces) of all active MCP servers.
2. Ensure the test fails if any tool description exceeds the Anthropic/Gemini 1024-character cap.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `McpServerToolLimits` tests all registered MCP servers.
- [ ] Tests fail for the known over-cap tools (e.g., `sync_all`).
- [ ] Test execution remains fast and runs within the standard unit test suite.

### Out of Scope
Fixing the over-cap descriptions is out of scope for this ticket. This ticket is purely about extending the enforcement test.
